title: Residuals: The Leftover Target
concept: A residual is the leftover difference between the true value and what the model predicted. In gradient boosting, these residuals become the actual training target for the next tree.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main idea of Gradient Boosting?

options.text: Training trees sequentially so each new tree corrects the errors of the previous ones.
Averaging the output of 1,000 independent trees trained all at once.
Using a single massive tree with infinite branches to fit the data.

exercises.explain: Correct! Gradient boosting trains trees in a sequence, with each new tree correcting the errors of the combination before it.

cards.title: Measuring the Gap
cards.body: If you guess a puppy's weight is 10 pounds, but it actually weighs 12 pounds, your guess is off by 2 pounds. That leftover 2 pounds isn't just an error — it is a precise target. To get the perfect answer, you just need to predict that leftover amount and add it to your first guess.

cards.title: What is a Residual?
cards.body: In machine learning, the difference between the actual correct answer and the model's current prediction is called a residual. It represents the leftover error. Formula-wise, it is simply: Residual = Actual - Predicted.

cards.title: The GPS Re-routing
cards.body: Imagine driving to a friend's house. You miss a turn and end up 3 miles away from the route. The GPS doesn't plan your entire trip from your home again. Instead, it calculates the route for the remaining 3 miles. The GPS focuses only on the leftover distance (the residual) to get you to your destination.

cards.title: Predicting High Scores
cards.body:
Let's predict a player's score of 100 points:
• Tree 1 predicts 80 points.
• The residual is 100 - 80 = +20.
• Tree 2 is trained to predict this +20.
• Tree 2 predicts +15.
• The new residual is 20 - 15 = +5.
• Tree 3 is trained to predict the leftover +5.

cards.title: Errors Are Not Garbage
cards.body: You might think errors are just bad outputs to be ignored. In gradient boosting, however, the residuals are the most important inputs! They tell the next tree exactly which data points are still being guessed incorrectly and by how much.

cards.title: Target vs. Residual
cards.body: What the decision trees are actually trying to predict:

left.text:
Predicts the original values.
e.g. Target = 100 points.
Often makes large, rough guesses.

right.text:
Predicts the leftover error.
e.g. Target = +20 points.
Fuses corrections onto Tree 1.

exercises.prompt: If a student's actual test score is 85 and our model predicts it will be 80, what is the residual?
exercises.explain: Exactly! Residual = Actual (85) - Predicted (80) = +5. The next tree will train to predict this +5.

exercises.prompt: True or false: In Gradient Boosting, the target for the second tree is the original actual value.
exercises.explain: False. The target for the second tree is the residual (the leftover error) of the first tree's prediction.

exercises.sentence: A residual is calculated as the actual value minus the ___ value.
exercises.explain: The residual is the difference between what actually happened (actual) and what the model guessed (predicted).

exercises.prompt: Which of these outputs represents a residual value?
exercises.explain: A residual is the leftover difference (+3.2 seconds) between actual (48.2) and predicted (45.0).

exercises.prompt: A model predicts a house is worth $300k. The actual price is $280k. What is the residual, and what will the next tree try to predict for this house?

options.text: The residual is -$20k, and the next tree will try to predict this -$20k correction.
The residual is +$20k, and the next tree will try to predict the original $280k.
The residual is -$20k, and the next tree will try to predict $300k.
The residual is zero, because the prediction was close enough.

exercises.explain: Perfect! Actual ($280k) - Predicted ($300k) = -$20k. The residual is negative, meaning the first tree guessed too high. The next tree will learn to predict -$20k to drag the prediction back down to the correct value!
